mnemonics: My Very Educated Mother Just Served Us Nine Pizzas: Version, Prev Hash, Merkle Root, Time, nBits, Nonce
keyPoints: The block header is the 80-byte metadata section at the beginning of a block.
It contains a cryptographic hash of the previous block, creating the immutable chain.
The Merkle Root is a hash of all transactions, efficiently securing the block's data.
commonMistakes: Confusing the block header (metadata) with the block body (transactions).
Forgetting that the Nonce is a simple number used for Proof-of-Work mining.
